title: PDF Page 1
content:
Plan summary :  
Realizing in taxi mode an overview of every common flight failure while also recording with 
an appropriate instrument the power and frequence of the electromagnetic field interne 
and externe with respect to the aircraft. While also having the battery system certified for T 
category of the DO – 160G section 20 concerning HIRF. 
 
Identification of the aircraft systems, with classification  
• Identification of all aircraft systems subject to HIRF influence will be performed. 
• Each system will be classified based on its criticality as defined in the Functional 
Hazard Assessment (FHA). 
• As per the FHA, the battery system exhibits a hazardous-level failure condition, 
classifying the system as Level 1 under AC 20-158B. 
• The battery system must therefore comply with DO-160G Section 20, Category T. 
 
 
HIRF environment for the aircraft and installed systems 
• The applicable HIRF environment for aircraft-level testing is Environment I or II per 
AC 20-158B Table 1, pending confirmation via measurement. 
• Using real-time measurements during taxi operations, the actual EMF exposure will 
be characterized. 
• This will determine whether the natural environment exceeds or matches these 
standardized test environments, potentially reducing the need for additional 
aircraft-level radiated testing.

title: PDF Page 2
content:
Verification methods  
A. In-Taxi Functional Testing 
• Functional verification of Level 1 systems (e.g., battery control, monitoring, flight-
critical avionics) during taxi operations. 
• Simulations of common failure modes, as identified in the FHA, will be used to 
validate system performance under real-world EMF conditions. 
• These tests assess operational robustness in environments typical of commercial 
airports, which often contain elevated EMF exposure. 
B. Electromagnetic Field Measurement 
• A spectrum analyzer will record internal and external EMF field strength and 
frequency while the aircraft is in taxi mode. 
• Objectives: 
o Quantify actual HIRF exposure at the airport during taxi. 
o Determine if this exposure is equal to or more severe than Environment I or II. 
o Analyze the frequency content to confirm coverage of critical bands, 
especially near battery system switching frequencies or known 
vulnerabilities. 
• If these conditions are met, and subject to regulatory agreement, this may justify a 
partial substitution of formal DO-160G radiated susceptibility tests (RS). 
C. Laboratory Susceptibility Testing 
• Battery system will undergo DO-160G Section 20 Category T testing, based on its 
hazardous-level classification. 
• Additional conducted susceptibility (CS) tests (DO-160G 20.6) will be performed on 
affected systems to assess vulnerability to cable-coupled HIRF signals. 
• These tests are critical to validate immunity to indirect HIRF effects not replicated in 
radiated test environments alone.
